evidence_quotes.quote: Currently companies are removing access to the products (Video Games) we purchase from them

paragraphs.text: Please consider including video games into the Digital Fairness Act. It is one of the largest industries in the digital age with the greatest reach among the youth and young adults whom may not show much interest in politics otherwise. Currently companies are removing access to the products (Video Games) we purchase from them, this is due to the unethical method these products are set up forcing the need for an internet connection to use and those servers being shut down when the product is no longer being supported. Most come with zero plans for end of life support, turning a perfectly functioning product into useless data. Hundreds of these products have already been discontinued solely because of laziness from the publishers and/or developers whom do not plan for the product's end of life state, which isn't all that difficult to do so anyway, it simply isn't profitable for them and therefore they do not. But that does not make it right. There is already an effort by us consumers to stop this, as seen in the below link of over a million EU signatures in support. https://eci.ec.europa.eu/045/public/#/screen/home
